'''Junior Langi''' (born 2 August 1981) is a former professional [[rugby league]] footballer who played in the 2000s for the [[St. George Illawarra Dragons|St. George Illawarra]], [[Melbourne Storm]], [[Parramatta Eels]] and [[Salford Red Devils]].

==Early life==
Born in [[Auckland, New Zealand]] with Niuean and Tongan heritage. Langi moved to Australia, educated at [[Trinity Catholic College, Auburn]], where he represented  [[Australia national schoolboys rugby league team|1999 Australian Schoolboys]].<ref>{{cite web|title=SportingPulse Homepage for Australian Secondary Schools Rugby League|url=http://www.sportingpulse.com/assoc_page.cgi?c=7-2130-0-0-0&sID=26424|accessdate=2008-10-10|publisher=SportingPulse|archive-date=31 January 2017|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20170131030043/http://websites.sportstg.com/assoc_page.cgi?c=7-2130-0-0-0&sID=26424|url-status=dead}}</ref>

==Playing career==
Langi made his first grade debut for [[St. George Illawarra Dragons|St. George]] against [[Canterbury-Bankstown Bulldogs|Canterbury]] in round 3 in the [[2000 NRL season]].  Langi then joined Melbourne and played with the club between 2001 and 2003 before joining Parramatta in 2004. Langi became a regular starter for Parramatta and made 16 appearances for the club.  Langi signed for [[Salford Red Devils|Salford]] in 2005. He retired from the sport in December 2006 due to an eye condition and returned home.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.parraeels.com.au/about/official-player-numbers/|title=Official Player Numbers|website=Parramatta Eels}}</ref>
